explanations.a: Incorrect. Edible oils are plant-based and do not contain cholesterol (cholesterol is only found in animal products).
explanations.b: Correct. Cheese is an animal product and contains cholesterol.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Beef is an animal product and contains cholesterol, but not the only source.
explanations.d: Incorrect because edible oil does not contain cholesterol.

questions.question: 2. Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) is called 'bad' cholesterol because:

answers.b: It can cause blockage in the arteries

explanations.a: Incorrect. LDL does not cause anemia.
explanations.b: Correct. LDL is called 'bad' cholesterol because it can build up in the walls of arteries, forming plaque that causes blockage (atherosclerosis) and increases risk of heart attack and stroke.
explanations.c: Incorrect. LDL does not directly alter heart rhythm.

questions.question: 3. High-density lipoprotein (HDL) is called 'good' cholesterol because:

answers.a: It keeps cholesterol from building up in the lining of the arteries
answers.c: It helps keep your blood from clotting

explanations.a: Correct. HDL is called 'good' cholesterol because it carries cholesterol away from the arteries and back to the liver, preventing plaque buildup in artery walls.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The body's production of HDL varies, but this is not the reason it is called 'good'.
explanations.c: Incorrect. HDL does not prevent blood clotting.

questions.question: 4. Besides high cholesterol, which of these is a major risk factor for heart disease?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Cigarette smoking is a risk factor, but not the only one.
explanations.b: Incorrect. High blood pressure is a risk factor, but not the only one.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Family history is a risk factor, but not the only one.
explanations.d: Correct. All of the above (cigarette smoking, high blood pressure, family history, and age) are major risk factors for heart disease.

questions.question: 5. Which of the following is a desirable total cholesterol level?

explanations.a: Correct. A desirable total cholesterol level is less than 200 mg/dL. Levels between 200-239 mg/dL are borderline high, and 240 mg/dL and above is high.
explanations.b: Incorrect. More than 200 mg/dL is considered borderline high or high.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Less than 300 mg/dL is not the standard desirable level.
